objective: Update the "What's New" section in README.md to reflect v4.5.0. First, read .glitchlab/audit.jsonl to identify what changed between v4.4.0 and v4.5.0. Then rewrite ONLY the "What's New in v4.3.0" section (and its subsections) to summarize the actual changes that landed. Keep the version number updates in __init__.py, identity.py, pyproject.toml, and tests/test_identity.py as-is — this task touches ONLY README.md. Preserve all other README content exactly. Do not alter the project description, installation instructions, usage examples, or any section outside "What's New."
commit_message: docs: update README What's New for v4.5.0
implementation_summary: Rewrote only the README.md "What's New" section to reflect the actual v4.5.0 release: synchronized version metadata, public exports, and identity test assertions, with a brief explanation of why alignment matters. Verified the README is readable and contains the updated v4.5.0 section while preserving the rest of the file.

data.description: Checks README.md's public-facing What's New section was updated to v4.5.0 and summarizes the version synchronization changes that landed.
